seoTitle: How to Add a Background Image in Webflow
metaDescription: Learn how to add and properly size a background image in Webflow using Cover and Contain presets, plus overlay tips.
intro: Background images are a quick way to add visual impact to a hero section or full-width block, but getting the sizing and alignment right takes a bit of know-how. This guide covers adding an image through Webflow's Backgrounds control, choosing between Cover and Contain, and layering overlays for readability. It's for designers building sections that need a strong visual backdrop.

steps.heading: Select the target container
steps.body: Click the section or div block where you want the background image to appear.

steps.heading: Open the Backgrounds panel
steps.body: In the right sidebar Style manager, expand the Backgrounds section and click the plus icon to add an image or gradient.

steps.heading: Upload or choose an image
steps.body: Click Select Image to open the asset library, then upload a new high-resolution image or pick an existing one from your project.

steps.heading: Apply sizing and alignment
steps.body: Choose Cover to fill the container completely or Contain to keep the whole image visible, then set alignment coordinates to anchor the focal point.

steps.heading: Add an overlay if needed
steps.body: Turn off image repeating with the tile switch, then layer a color overlay or gradient over the image to keep any text on top readable.

faq.question: What's the difference between Cover and Contain?
faq.answer: Cover scales the image to completely fill the container, sometimes cropping edges, while Contain keeps the full image visible but may leave empty space.

faq.question: Why does my background image look stretched?
faq.answer: This typically happens when the sizing is set incorrectly, so switch to Cover or Contain instead of a fixed stretch setting.

faq.question: How do I keep text readable over a busy image?
faq.answer: Add a semi-transparent color overlay or gradient on top of the image so any text sitting above it has enough contrast.

faq.question: Will a large background image slow down my page?
faq.answer: Yes, so it's worth compressing images before uploading and relying on Webflow's automatic optimization where possible.
